title: manulea campanulata
intro: Manulea campanulata, also known as the bell-flowered manulea, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The leaves and flowers of the plant can be prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies.
